---
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Hubbard Hall stakeholder analysis

This book explores Hubbard Hall's stakeholders — **donors, patrons, and class-takers**. All
charts are aggregates; no individual is identified. Data sources, definitions, and methods are in
the [Methodology](99-methodology.html) appendix.

**Headline findings**

```{python}
#| label: setup
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from hh.io import read_parquet
from hh.viz import style, CATEGORICAL, BLUE, SEQUENTIAL_BLUE, INK, MUTED

households = read_parquet("processed", "households_summary.parquet")
regs = read_parquet("processed", "registrations_enriched.parquet")

# headline figures (computed, not hardcoded)
_order = ["[0, 1)", "[1, 5)", "[5, 10)", "[10, 20)", "[20, ∞)"]
_d = (
    households[households["is_donor"]]
    .groupby("distance_band", observed=True)["total_donations"]
    .sum()
    .reindex(_order)
    .fillna(0)
)
_c = _d.cumsum() / _d.sum()
within5, within10, within20 = (
    f"{_c.loc[b]:.0%}" for b in ("[1, 5)", "[5, 10)", "[10, 20)")
)
n_hh = f"{len(households):,}"
n_donors = f"{int(households['is_donor'].sum()):,}"
n_patrons = f"{int(households['is_patron'].sum()):,}"
n_class = f"{int(households['is_class_taker'].sum()):,}"
n_donor_class = f"{int((households['is_donor'] & households['is_class_taker']).sum()):,}"
n_all3 = f"{int((households['is_donor'] & households['is_patron'] & households['is_class_taker']).sum()):,}"

from hh.analytics.productions import is_excluded, count_succeeded_attendees

_near = ["[0, 1)", "[1, 5)", "[5, 10)"]
_cl = regs[regs["event_majorcat"] == "class"].dropna(subset=["distance_band"])
class_w10 = f"{_cl['distance_band'].isin(_near).mean():.0%}"
_th = regs[regs["event_minorcat"] == "theater"].dropna(subset=["distance_band"])
theater_w10 = f"{_th['distance_band'].isin(_near).mean():.0%}"
_perf = regs[(regs["event_majorcat"] == "performance") & ~regs["event_name"].map(is_excluded)].copy()
_perf["attendees"] = _perf["tickets"].map(count_succeeded_attendees)
_don = _perf.merge(households[["id", "is_donor"]], on="id", how="left")
donor_att_share = f"{_don.loc[_don['is_donor'].fillna(False), 'attendees'].sum() / _don['attendees'].sum():.0%}"
```

- **Donors are local.** About **`{python} within10`** of donated dollars come from within 10 miles
  of Hubbard Hall (`{python} within5` within 5 miles; `{python} within20` within 20 miles).
- **Theater draws on Saturday; music on Sunday.** But within a theater run that plays
  Fri/Sat/Sun, Saturday leads only because it usually has two shows — per performance the three
  days are essentially tied, and Saturday matinees are the weekend's weakest slot. The ranking
  is shifting: since 2021, Sunday has been the best-attended day per performance in nearly every
  full-weekend run. Details are in
  [When people come to Hubbard Hall](02-timing.html).
- **Classes are local; performances are regional.** `{python} class_w10` of class registrations
  come from within 10 miles, versus `{python} theater_w10` for theater — and Sunday draws the
  farthest-traveling theater audience. See
  [How far do people travel to Hubbard Hall?](03-travel-distance.html).
- **Strong overlap:** of `{python} n_hh` households, `{python} n_donors` donate,
  `{python} n_patrons` attend performances, and `{python} n_class` take classes;
  `{python} n_donor_class` both donate and take classes, `{python} n_all3` do all three.
- **Donor households fill most seats:** they account for `{python} donor_att_share` of all
  performance attendance. See
  [What performances do donors attend?](05-donor-attendance.html).
